role_delineation_check: The Social Worker is responsible for selecting the Home Health Agency (HHA) and coordinating the clinical bedside teaching for medication management, while the Patient Navigator (PN) audits demographics in Atlantis, educates the family on post-discharge expectations, and flags the specific caregiver training gap to the SW for resolution.
narrative_summary: 78-year-old Eleanor, recovering from a CHF exacerbation, was medically cleared for discharge, but her daughter experienced significant anxiety regarding the complex new diuretic schedule. This caregiver training gap created a barrier to transition, as the family felt unprepared to monitor Eleanor's daily weights and medication compliance without professional guidance. The Patient Navigator acted as a liaison by documenting the daughter's low sentiment score and flagging the specific training needs to the Social Worker, ensuring that a bedside teaching session was completed prior to the first home visit.
demographic_audit_note: Audited the Atlantis referral against the facility face sheet; confirmed the patient's DOB (05/14/1946) and verified the primary contact number was updated from the patient's landline to the daughter's mobile number to ensure reachability for the pulse call.
home_vs_ltc_goal: Home with HHA and specialized wound/CHF monitoring
v_card_flyer_status: Delivered the program flyer to the bedside and successfully inserted the V-Card into the daughter’s smartphone, explaining that the 'Healing Partners' caller ID would appear for all post-discharge care coordination calls.
pre_dc_pulse_call: Conducted the 24-hour pre-discharge pulse call; the daughter confirmed that while she participated in the bedside teaching, her anxiety remains high. Sentiment score recorded as 'Anxious/Reluctant' and flagged to the SW to ensure the HHA nurse prioritizes the medication review.
atlantis_final_sync: Documented the discharge date of 10/18 and the confirmed first home visit for 10/19 in Atlantis after the SW verified that the HHA had accepted the case and assigned a nurse.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Patient Eleanor appears in the Atlantis queue following a CHF exacerbation; PN begins initial demographic audit against the facility records.
The daughter expresses high levels of panic regarding the titration of diuretics at home, stating she does not feel competent in managing the clinical regimen.
PN flags the daughter's sentiment score and specific training gap to the Social Worker in Atlantis and waits for the clinical team to schedule bedside teaching.
The Social Worker confirms via the Atlantis portal that the HHA has accepted the referral and the bedside teaching session has been completed by the facility nurse.

format_2_triples.situation: The daughter is visibly overwhelmed by the new CHF medication list during the initial meeting.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educate the family on the Day 1 home expectations and the role of the Medical Assistant in supporting the transition.

format_2_triples.situation: The HHA acceptance status remains 'Pending' in the Atlantis portal 48 hours before the expected discharge.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Flag the HHA acceptance stall to the Social Worker and wait for the vendor confirmation to be updated in the system.

format_2_triples.situation: There is a discrepancy between the patient's home address in the EMR and the face sheet.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Verify the correct discharge address with the daughter and document the update in the Atlantis demographic field.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Upon learning the daughter is refusing discharge due to medication fears, the PN documents the delay in Atlantis and waits for the Social Worker to facilitate a family care conference with the nursing staff.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the professional boundary where clinical education and discharge readiness are managed by the SW and clinical team, while the PN maintains the record.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN verifies the daughter's specific concerns regarding the CHF scale and diuretic schedule, flags these as a 'Sentiment_Readiness_Gap' to the SW, and educates the family on how the HHA and MA will collaborate during the first 24 hours home.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This uses liaison verbs to bridge the communication gap between the family and the SW without overstepping into clinical instruction.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN calls the HHA intake coordinator directly to request that they send a nurse with specific CHF experience for the first visit to help calm the daughter's nerves regarding the medication schedule.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This violates the 'No-Vendor Rule' by contacting the HHA intake directly and attempts to solve a clinical staffing issue which is the responsibility of the SW.
